CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Jun-08-2015 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-29-2015 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar May-18-2015 Continued To Jun-19-15 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 613

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion
Off Calendar Apr-27-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Malcolm Leader-Picone, of Bartlett, Leader-Picone & Young, LLP, Tel. 510.444.2404, for plaintiffs;
Jennifer L. Stark and Gay Grunfeld, of Rosen Bien Galvan & Grunfeld LLP, Tel. 415.433.6830, for defendants;
No other appearances.

RULING: Judge Kahn disclosed that he knows Cathy Rosen, wife of first named counsel for defendant, who passed away. This will not affect the ability of the Court to hear this matter. The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT NADER SHABAHANGI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Denied. As to the statute of limitations argument, there is a triable issue of material fact as to when the alleged breach occurred. Plaintiff states that he received a payment from Defendant in late December 2010. (Declaration of Ali Shabahangi, par. 8.) This statement does not contradict Plaintiff's response to Defendant's request for production. A reasonable jury could find that no transaction record exists for a number of reasons. As to the statute of frauds argument, the critical inquiry is whether by the contract's own terms it would be impossible to perform within a year. (Hollywood Motion Picture Equipment Co. v. Furer (1940) 16 Cal.2d 184, 187.) Under either party's characterization of how long the agreement was supposed to last (as set forth in Undisputed Material Fact 38), it would not have been impossible for the contract to be performed within one year. For example, had Plaintiff sold the property within one year after Defendant re-conveyed it to him, the contract would have been fully performed in less than one year. Order signed. Judge Harold Kahn; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Denise Doucette, CSR No. 5963; Dept. 302.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion
Off Calendar Apr-01-2015 Continued To Apr-27-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Atty. GAY CROSTHWAIT GRUNFELD of Rosen Bien Galvan & Grunfeld LLP, Tel No. (415) 433-6830 appearing for Defendant Nader Shabahangi.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court modified its tentative ruling and ordered as follows: The Court is in receipt of the courtesy copies delivered by parties to the Court on March 27, 2015. However, the courtesy copies were not received in enough advance time for the Court to rule on the motion. DEFENDANT NADER SHABAHANGI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to April 27, 2015 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
